idea.hook: Did you know a *moon* could be bigger than a *planet*?
facts: Jupiter's moon Ganymede is the largest moon in our Solar System.
That makes it bigger than the planet Mercury, which has a diameter of 4,879 kilometers (3,032 miles).
It's also the ONLY moon known to generate its own magnetic field, just like Earth!
idea.payoff: Ganymede: A planetary powerhouse, just not orbiting the Sun!
idea.title: Moon BIGGER Than A Planet?! 🤯🪐

scenes.text: A MOON bigger than a PLANET?
scenes.visual: Striking deep space background with subtle nebulae. A question mark graphic pulses briefly.

scenes.text:
Ganymede: Largest Moon in Our Solar System!
(5,262 km / 3,269 miles across)
scenes.visual: A detailed, textured image of Ganymede rotating slowly against a backdrop of distant stars. Zoom in slightly on Ganymede.

scenes.text:
BIGGER than Planet Mercury!
(Ganymede: 5,262 km > Mercury: 4,879 km)
scenes.visual: Split screen comparison: Ganymede on left, Mercury on right, clearly showing Ganymede as larger. Animated size rings or indicators.

scenes.text: The ONLY Moon with its own Magnetic Field!
scenes.visual: Ganymede with visible, swirling magnetic field lines illustrated around it, similar to Earth's aurora, glowing subtly.
A majestic, full-screen view of Ganymede, slowly rotating, with subtle light glints emphasizing its surface features. Text pulses with a subtle glow.
